title: DDOM Document
description: Schema for Declarative Document Object Model (DDOM) component files. A DDOM document is a JSON object that declaratively describes a reactive web component: its structure (DOM tree), styling, reactive state ($defs), and a reference to its companion JavaScript handler file.

$schema.description: URI identifying the DDOM dialect version. Enables schema-aware IDE tooling.

$id.description: Component identifier string. Used by tooling and the builder.

$handlers.description: Relative path or URL to the companion .js ES module file that exports event handlers. Follows ES module specifier rules — IDE CTRL-click navigation works natively.

$defs.description: Signal and handler declarations for this component. $-prefixed keys are signals; plain keys are handler declarations.

DefsMap.description: Map of signal declarations, computed signals, handler declarations, and prototype namespaces.

propertyNames.description: Signal names conventionally use the $ prefix; handler names do not.

DefEntry.description: A single $defs entry: a signal, computed signal, handler declaration, or prototype namespace.

StateSignalDef.description: A reactive state signal. Wraps the default value in a Signal.State at runtime.

ComputedSignalDef.description: A read-only computed signal. Evaluated as a JSONata expression. Re-evaluates whenever any declared $dep changes.

$compute.description: JSONata expression string. Has access to dep signal values by their key name (without #/$defs/ prefix).

$deps.description: Explicit dependency list. Each entry is a $ref string pointing to a $defs signal.

HandlerDef.description: Declares that a function with this key must be exported from the $handlers module. The compiler validates that the export exists.

examples.description: Fires when element connects to the DOM

PrototypeDef.description: A Web API namespace signal. The $prototype key identifies which Web API to wrap.

$prototype.description: Web API constructor name identifying the namespace handler.

timing.description: Execution timing for Request prototypes. "server" bakes the response at build time; "client" (default) fetches at runtime.

manual.description: If true, the Request will not auto-fetch. Call .fetch() manually.

debounce.description: Debounce interval in milliseconds for auto-fetching Request prototypes.

body.description: Request body. Objects are JSON-serialized automatically.

ElementDef.description: A DDOM element definition. Maps directly to a DOM element.

onclick.description: Event handler for the "click" event. Must be a $ref to a declared handler.

ondblclick.description: Event handler for the "dblclick" event. Must be a $ref to a declared handler.

onmousedown.description: Event handler for the "mousedown" event. Must be a $ref to a declared handler.

onmouseup.description: Event handler for the "mouseup" event. Must be a $ref to a declared handler.

onmouseover.description: Event handler for the "mouseover" event. Must be a $ref to a declared handler.

onmouseout.description: Event handler for the "mouseout" event. Must be a $ref to a declared handler.

onmousemove.description: Event handler for the "mousemove" event. Must be a $ref to a declared handler.

onkeydown.description: Event handler for the "keydown" event. Must be a $ref to a declared handler.

onkeyup.description: Event handler for the "keyup" event. Must be a $ref to a declared handler.

onkeypress.description: Event handler for the "keypress" event. Must be a $ref to a declared handler.

oninput.description: Event handler for the "input" event. Must be a $ref to a declared handler.

onchange.description: Event handler for the "change" event. Must be a $ref to a declared handler.

onsubmit.description: Event handler for the "submit" event. Must be a $ref to a declared handler.

onreset.description: Event handler for the "reset" event. Must be a $ref to a declared handler.

onfocus.description: Event handler for the "focus" event. Must be a $ref to a declared handler.

onblur.description: Event handler for the "blur" event. Must be a $ref to a declared handler.

onscroll.description: Event handler for the "scroll" event. Must be a $ref to a declared handler.

onresize.description: Event handler for the "resize" event. Must be a $ref to a declared handler.

onload.description: Event handler for the "load" event. Must be a $ref to a declared handler.

onunload.description: Event handler for the "unload" event. Must be a $ref to a declared handler.

onerror.description: Event handler for the "error" event. Must be a $ref to a declared handler.

oncontextmenu.description: Event handler for the "contextmenu" event. Must be a $ref to a declared handler.

onwheel.description: Event handler for the "wheel" event. Must be a $ref to a declared handler.

ondrag.description: Event handler for the "drag" event. Must be a $ref to a declared handler.

ondragstart.description: Event handler for the "dragstart" event. Must be a $ref to a declared handler.

ondragend.description: Event handler for the "dragend" event. Must be a $ref to a declared handler.

ondragover.description: Event handler for the "dragover" event. Must be a $ref to a declared handler.

ondrop.description: Event handler for the "drop" event. Must be a $ref to a declared handler.

ontouchstart.description: Event handler for the "touchstart" event. Must be a $ref to a declared handler.

ontouchmove.description: Event handler for the "touchmove" event. Must be a $ref to a declared handler.

ontouchend.description: Event handler for the "touchend" event. Must be a $ref to a declared handler.

onpointerdown.description: Event handler for the "pointerdown" event. Must be a $ref to a declared handler.

onpointermove.description: Event handler for the "pointermove" event. Must be a $ref to a declared handler.

onpointerup.description: Event handler for the "pointerup" event. Must be a $ref to a declared handler.

onpointercancel.description: Event handler for the "pointercancel" event. Must be a $ref to a declared handler.

ChildrenValue.description: Children of an element. Either a static array of element definitions, or an Array namespace configuration for dynamic mapped lists.

oneOf.description: Static array of child element definitions.

ArrayNamespace.description: Dynamic mapped list. Renders one child per item from the source array. Re-renders when the items signal changes.

items.description: Data source. A $ref to a signal or a static array.

map.description: Element template rendered for each item. May reference $map/item and $map/index.

filter.description: $ref to a handler function used to filter items.

sort.description: $ref to a handler function used to sort items.

SwitchDef.description: Dynamic component switching. The cases object maps signal values to element definitions. The rendered case is replaced whenever the signal changes.

SwitchNode.description: An element that renders one of several case components based on a signal value.

cases.description: Map of signal value strings to element definitions or external $ref components.

StyleObject.description: CSS style definition. camelCase property names follow CSSOM convention. Keys starting with :, ., &, or [ are treated as nested CSS selectors.

AttributesObject.description: Non-standard HTML attributes and ARIA attributes. Rendered as element.setAttribute(k,v).

PropsObject.description: Explicit prop passing at a component boundary. Keys must correspond to $defs entries in the referenced component. Values may be static literals or $ref bindings.

RefObject.description: A $ref binding. Resolves to a signal (reactive) or plain value (static).

AnyRef.description: A $ref URI string. May be internal, external file, or global namespace.

InternalRef.description: Reference to a signal or handler in the current component's $defs.

ExternalRef.description: Reference to an external DDOM component file.

ExternalComponentRef.description: Object form of an external component reference, with optional $props.

GlobalRef.description: Reference to a window or document global property.

MapRef.description: Reference to the current Array map iteration context.

ElementPropertyValue.description: Any valid value for a DOM element property.

TagName.description: HTML tag name. Custom elements (containing a hyphen) are registered as autonomous custom elements per the Web Components specification.
